ddt:Target.Name: BVR6L_X62_ZA4_v1.8
ddt:Target.Description:
ZF Automated Manual Transmission Applications: BVR6L - X62 Software: Date: 02.02.2011 Author: DCMAP-RTR / UET TA API: RO RTR NG2 1 04 ------------------------------------------------------------------------ Reference document: DCMAP_RTR_2009_177_X62_M9T_ZA4_DUD_v1.8 ----------------------------------------------------------------------- Updates performed on V 1.8:
- Added unit [mm] for DIDs 2DB7 and 2D88 - Modified DID 2DBE from signed to unsigned and erased values for N and R - Updated Auto-Ident SW list. - Added HomologationNumber data - Added request $0904 for reading the OBD Vehicle Identification data

Data.Name: Reverse Light Diagnostic Status Shorted to Ground
Reverse Light Diagnostic Status Shorted to High
UVPE_HS (=UKL30_HS + filter) Internal Voltage at Engines
UVDD12 (internal 12V for H-Bridges and power supply miscellaneous)
Position of Reverse Gear EOL Parameter
Gearbox Output Speed for Freeze Frame

Item.Text: ABS and ASR and Retarder Installed

Request.Name: DataRead.Identification.RenaultR2
Request.Comment: ReadDataByLocalIdentifier : Use this request if ECU use Coding Renault R2 or 5DIGITS

Request.Name: TesterPresent.WithResponse
Request.Comment: This request enable P2P tester present for tool (Screen, IOctl, ...)

Request.Name: StartDiagnosticSession.Default
Request.Comment: Standard definition for DDT2000 automatic screen

DataItem.Name: Demand of gear engaged 1st optional control parameter
Demand of gear engaged 2nd optional control parameter

Request.Name: ClearDiagnosticInformation.All
Request.Comment: The ClearDiagnosticInformation service is used by the tool to clear diagnostic information in an ECUs memory

Request.Name: ClearFailures
Request.Comment: The ClearDiagnosticInformation service is used by the tool to clear diagnostic information in an ECUs memory

I/O control - Demand of gearchange status

Test.Description: Engine does not react on TCU's torque command
Invalid Data Of Engine Speed Received from ECM_TorqueControl message
ErgonomicPedal Fail Signal Received from ECM_to_AT Message

Device.Name: TCM Processor
Device.Mnemonic: TCU internal Voltage For Micro Controller

Test.Description: Differential speed between rear wheels and gearbox output detected
multiple detection of unexpected clutch slipping (checked by nEng & nOut of gearbox)
Clutch Position Duty Cycle Plausibility Staring Off Phase
short cut ground of clutch sensor voltage (U_AU)
multiple clutch torque plausibility error because of wrong torque, transmitted by the clutch
Clutch over heat warning buzzer has been ignored and clucth temperature has been more high

Device.Name: Gear Lever X-Y Position Sensor Circuit

Test.Description: Shift lever fixed in interim position

Test.Description: one or more EOL select measure values are out of tolerance

Device.Name: End of line - Gear shift actuator

Test.Description: one or more EOL shift measure values are out of tolerance
voltage too low at electrical motor shift (EDRIVE_SHIFT)
voltage too high at electrical motor shift (EDRIVE_SHIFT)

Label.Text: Initial conditions: vehicle in standstill, gearbox in neutral, engine OFF
Initial conditions: engine on, vehicle in standstill, gearbox in neutral, brake pedal pressed
Note: 30s Power Latch must be performed between each learning operation
Initial conditions: vehicle in standstill, gearbox in neutral, engine OFF
Initial conditions: Gearbox in Neutral, Vehicle in standstill condition, Engine OFF
Initial conditions: Gearbox in Neutral, Vehicle in standstill condition, no active failures at clutch valves, Engine OFF
Initial conditions: Gearbox in Neutral, Vehicle in standstill condition, Engine OFF
NOTE: for engaging the reverse gear, requested gear should be 1st and requested direction should be backward
